Q&A credit to 滑蛋豬仔飯

Q: 如果無BC又想係英國打工係咪好難?

A:
通常會比working visa一係大公司
D細公司仔都見過有批不過當然會少D

老老實實渣BNO/特區係就係難D不過都唔係無可能
都識得有人搞得掂個visa
以前有同學會係cv度寫明話要working visa
如果間公司都肯in你既話多數即係肯批
唔洗浪費大家時間
大小公司都有 試下無妨

再唔係學其他巴打講
申請左working holiday做住先都未嘗不可
當然之後整唔整到tier 2 working visa就唔敢講
不過起碼都有2年比你係度做下野儲下經驗先

如果做開比較專業行業既朋友其實拎左working holiday visa先，再轉tier 2既機會都幾樂觀，都識幾個朋友都係咁，好似都係做design / I.T.之類 (by 賽麗娜Serena)


Q: 想問下英國見工係咪同香港一樣都係上網報?

A:
如果你話搵工過程其實同香港差唔多
睇你咩工啦
普通黎講大公司既話都係cv cover letter -> online test -> phone in-> assessment centre ( 可以好多round) -> final in
細公司就少幾個步驟咁


Q: 想問下係咪bno 同特區都可以fg 一年tier5 先，唔洗特登要搵肯比tier2 既公司住

A:
你英國畢業定香港想過去？ 唔係英國畢業唔肯定當地申請有無唔同但BNO 隨時可以申請tier 5 重有兩年，特區要抽架，都係兩年。
當地申請可能短d , 我當初因爲公司未令到可以簽visa 既licence 就幫我申請住tier 5 先，得特區又過左抽籤所以係visa 公司幫我係英國申請，但咁就得一年.. 無深究點解。
但全部香港申請過去既都有兩年。 (by 花生哥)

Q: 下年打算去working holiday 會唔會好難搵工同搵地方住（想住反係zoom1,2) 聽人講就話最好係香港搵定先去係好定唔好 我就做開酒吧個邊會唔會香港人 真心求教

A:
英國bar tender既需求量其實好大 因為佢pub既數量基本上同香港7仔有得揮 甚至係正常既餐廳入面都會有間pub仔/bar比人淨飲野吹水
所以你有經驗根本唔洗驚 去到英國任何一個地方都一定搵到野做

講我自己經驗啦 我就做好typical既西餐廳既 入面全部鬼佬
因為要升supervisor 所以gm要求我識哂餐廳所有部份 包括floor hatch同bar
Bar嗰邊我完全係一張白紙 連用咖啡機都唔識 基本上淨係識開水喉倒果汁呢d白痴都識做既野
去到依家講緊一個在既時間 叫做忙起上黎可以打哂三邊 聽日冇人返比人編左去自己睇bar

我覺得做bar最緊要係識同客吹水 尤其係餐廳bar
好似我地咁 係住宅區 長期都有熟客日日黎打"等" 真係日日風雨不改黎報到
黎到冇野做咪同bar tender吹水囉 所以你一定要好吹得

其餘都冇乜野擔心 驚咪去indeed search下囉 一大炸請緊人 (by 小野屋杏)

Q: 英國黎緊暑假畢業，準備搵工 （宜家4月中係咪太遲)

A: 唔遲
雖然有啲係截左
但仲有好多
留學生有優勢 就係唔怕relocation
成個英國既工你都apply得
宜家真係開始send cv都唔會太遲既
好多大公司如果唔係grad scheme既話其實一路都請緊人
以前好多同學都係8/9月先有offer
有D直頭下年1月先有工開
所以唔洗擔心既肯踏出第一步搵工就ok (by 馬壯FN2)

If you are getting an UK Tier 2 (Work Permit) visa and have British National (Overseas) status, you should get your UK Tier 2 visa using your BN(O) passport. This is because -

1) BN(O) passport holders with a long term UK visa do not need to register with the local police while HKSAR passport holders, as Chinese nationals, are required to register with the local police.

2) BN(O) passport holders with long term UK visas are treated as Commonwealth citizens in the UK. He/She can register to vote in all UK elections. HKSAR passport holders cannot register to vote in any UK elections. Being on the electoral roll helps you to build up your own credit history in the UK.

3) BN(O) passport holders can apply for British civil service jobs open to Commonwealth citizens once he/she has Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) while HKSAR passport holders cannot apply for British civil service jobs until he/she has become a British Citizen.

4) BN(O) passport holders can apply for registration to become a British Citizen using form B(OTA), this is a cheaper method than naturalisation. HKSAR passport holder must naturalise in order to get British Citizenship.

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/application-to-register-as-a-british-citizen-form-bota

A number of points for those of you who got British Citizenship via your parent and living in Hong Kong -

1) You are very likely to be a British Citizen by descent. This means that your child born outside of the UK will not automatically receive British Citizenship.

2) You can move back to the UK at any time you wanted. All you need is a valid British Citizen passport.

3) You cannot get UK welfare benefit until you lived in the UK for three months.

4) However, you can use the National Health Service (NHS) as soon as you are in the UK.

5) You will need to get a National Insurance (NI) Number. You can only apply for a NI number in the UK. Please see link below for details -

https://www.gov.uk/apply-national-insurance-number

6) You will not be entitle to UK 'home' student fees until you lived in the UK for at lease three years before the start of the course.

7) If you want to give birth in the UK in order to get British Citizenship for your child, I would suggest you to do so in Northern Ireland (Belfast for example). A child born in Northern Ireland with at lease one British Citizen parent is also entitle to Irish citizenship (as well as British Citizenship). Irish citizenship remains an EU citizenship after Brexit, which means that the child have the freedom of movement in the EU.

8) Banking - you can open UK bank accounts in Hong Kong with the HSBC or the Bank of East Asia. Please note that HSBC will charge a fee.

You will need ILR before getting British Citizenship.

The only 隱藏版upgrade BC is for a BN(O) passport holder, to join the British Armed Forces as a Commonwealth citizen. Once you have served in the British Armed Forces for four years, you can get ILR (UK immigration rule 276O)

Requirements for indefinite leave to remain in the United Kingdom as a foreign or Commonwealth citizen discharged from HM Forces

276O. The requirements for indefinite leave to remain in the United Kingdom as a foreign or Commonwealth citizen discharged from HM Forces are that the applicant:

(i) has completed at least four years’ service with HM Forces; and

(ii) was discharged from HM Forces on completion of engagement; and

(iii) was not discharged from HM Forces more than 2 years prior to the date on which the application is made unless they are applying following a grant of limited leavebto remain under paragraph 276QA; and

(iv) is not in the UK in breach of immigration laws except that, where paragraph 39E of these Rules applies, any current period of overstaying will be disregarded; and

(v) does not fall for refusal under the general grounds for refusal.

https://www.gov.uk/guidance/immigration-rules/immigration-rules-part-7-other-categories#pt7hmforces

You cannot joined the British Armed Forces if you only hold a HKSAR passport.

More points -

1) You can exchange your Hong Kong driving licence to an UK one without test in the UK as long as you pass your driving test in Hong Kong and you lived in the UK for less than 5 years.

https://www.facebook.com/HongkongerinUK/posts/861886470578180:0

2) You can renew your British passport (whether it is a British Citizen or British National (Overseas) passport) in Hong Kong online.

https://www.gov.uk/overseas-passports

The thread (『薪火post』BNO 續期資訊(12)討論別人的續期態度) below gives you all the information you need to renew your British passport in Hong Kong -

1. Those who are 'settled' in the UK and meet the main residence requirements

In order to qualify for 'home' fees under this category, you must meet all of the following criteria:

(a) you must be settled in the UK on the first day of the first academic year of the course; and

(b) you must be ordinarily resident in the UK on the first day of the first academic year of the course; and

(c) you must also have been ordinarily resident in the UK and Islands for the full three year period before the first day of the first academic year of the course - eg, if your course begins in October 2017, you must have been ordinarily resident in the UK and Islands from 1 September 2014 to 31 August 2017; and

(d) the main purpose for your residence in the UK and Islands must not have been to receive full-time education during any part of that three-year period.

Note: It is not necessary to have had settled immigration status in the UK for the full three years. You only need to show that you have it on, at the latest, the first day of the first academic year of the course.

東網透視：憑一支單簧管　香港仔入選御林軍

http://hk.on.cc/hk/bkn/cnt/news/20170812/bkn-20170812093047180-0812_00822_001.html

以香港第一人身份成爲英國御林軍樂手，David憶述，源於幾年前一次聚餐時偶遇英國陸軍音樂總監，對方提起英國御林軍樂隊招募新隊員，他持有英國國民(海外)護照(BNO)身份也可參與招聘考試，故決定一試。軍樂隊考試分兩部分，體能為先，音樂第二。對於當年33歲的他來說，體能測試是極大的挑戰，要10分半鐘內跑完2.4公里，亦要做掌上壓及仰臥起坐，首輪面試因跑步不達標而失敗。但他並沒有放棄，返港苦操3、4個月後再接受挑戰，2013年過關斬將通過所有測試，成功取錄。

身為英皇室的御林軍，薪酬福利令人關注，David則不予透露，僅指薪金水平較香港警察低一些，除普通的醫療和交通福利外，最優厚是住房福利，他與同事在倫敦市中心合住一間800至900平方呎單位，月租只需30英鎊，僅相當於300港元，相信令不少香港青年人羨慕。他現長居英國，還有一年便可入籍，目標是繼續在軍隊攀升，成爲終身職業。

Please note that a member of the British Armed Force who are subject to Service Law is exempted from UK immigration control.

Easiest way -

東網透視：憑一支單簧管　香港仔入選御林軍

http://hk.on.cc/hk/bkn/cnt/news/20170812/bkn-20170812093047180-0812_00822_001.html

以香港第一人身份成爲英國御林軍樂手，David憶述，源於幾年前一次聚餐時偶遇英國陸軍音樂總監，對方提起英國御林軍樂隊招募新隊員，他持有英國國民(海外)護照(BNO)身份也可參與招聘考試，故決定一試。軍樂隊考試分兩部分，體能為先，音樂第二。對於當年33歲的他來說，體能測試是極大的挑戰，要10分半鐘內跑完2.4公里，亦要做掌上壓及仰臥起坐，首輪面試因跑步不達標而失敗。但他並沒有放棄，返港苦操3、4個月後再接受挑戰，2013年過關斬將通過所有測試，成功取錄。

身為英皇室的御林軍，薪酬福利令人關注，David則不予透露，僅指薪金水平較香港警察低一些，除普通的醫療和交通福利外，最優厚是住房福利，他與同事在倫敦市中心合住一間800至900平方呎單位，月租只需30英鎊，僅相當於300港元，相信令不少香港青年人羨慕。他現長居英國，還有一年便可入籍，目標是繼續在軍隊攀升，成爲終身職業。

Please note that a member of the British Armed Force who are subject to Service Law is exempted from UK immigration control.

http://www.army.mod.uk/welfare-support/23209.aspx

20% VAT is applicable on most products. In the UK, there is a spirit duty of £28.74 per litre of pure alcohol on top of VAT.

https://www.gov.uk/tax-on-shopping/alcohol-tobacco

So the spirit duty of a litre bottle of 40% ABV vodka is 40% of £28.74, which is £11.50.

Hong Kong tax spirits in a different manner - the Hong Kong customs applies a 100% tax on the value of the spirit - so the more expensive a bottle of spirit is, the higher the duty.

http://www.customs.gov.hk/en/trade_facilitation/dutiable/types/

In the case of a 70cl bottle of Quinta Ruban (46% ABV) - it cost £50 in the UK.

https://www.glenmorangie.com/en-uk/our-whiskies/the-quinta-ruban

If my calculations are correct, UK customs would have charged £9.25 in spirit duty. A final price of £50 including VAT means that it is about £41 pre VAT. So the 'real' cost of the whiskey is about £31.75 with VAT & duty at about £19.

In Hong Kong, on the other hand, the importer will need to pay HK$317.50 (based on £1=HK$10) in import duties alone, plus higher transportation fees.

Someone with a Portuguese passport or ID card can exercise EU freedom of movement before Brexit (英國脫離歐盟) in March 2019.

This means that you, the Portuguese national with either a Portuguese passport or ID card, can work in the UK without the need of a visa. All you need is a National Insurance (NI) number from the Department for Works and Pensions. You can only get a NI number in person in the UK. Please see link below for details -

https://www.gov.uk/apply-national-insurance-number

Under current EU rules, you will be granted UK permanent residence status after working in the UK for 5 years. You can get British Citizenship a year after getting UK permanent residence.

If you want to work in the UK, I would suggest you to move to the UK quickly and certainly before Brexit.

If you are given leave to enter with an expiry date, then you are still subject to UK immigration control. Under section 4(2) of the British Nationality Act 1981, in order to register as a British Citizen, one of the requirement [section 4(2)(c)] is -

that he was not at any time in the period of twelve months so ending subject under the immigration laws to any restriction on the period for which he might remain in the United Kingdom

https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1981/61

Once you got Indefinite Leave to Remain in the UK, you will meet the requirement for section 4(2)(c). As a BN(O) passport holder, you can register to become a British Citizen under section 4(2) of the British Nationality Act 1981 using form B(OTA) -

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/application-to-register-as-a-british-citizen-form-bota

It is cheaper to register to become a British Citizen using form B(OTA) than naturalisation using form AN - £1,163 for registration vs £1,282 for naturalisation in 2017 prices.

https://www.gov.uk/government/uploads/system/uploads/attachment_data/file/652184/nationality_master_fees_leaflet_2017_update_sept_2017.pdf

2) 賣春 (Spouse visa)
3) 賣命 (Joining British Armed Forces)

Problem is that the UK got to spend a lot more money compare to Hong Kong on the follow things -

1) Military - the UK is committed to spend at lease 2% of her GDP in defence as part of the NATO target. Hong Kong, on the other hand, has no military spending as the Central People's government in Peking pays for Hong Kong's defence.

2) UK Embassies and High Commissions - the UK has a large network of embassies, high commissions and consulates around the world. The Hong Kong only has a dozen trade offices around the world.

3) Welfare spending - as part of the post WW2 settlement, the UK government pays out a lot of money in welfare spending compared to Hong Kong. For example, UK full pension is worth £122.30 per week while the Hong Kong Old Age Living Allowance (長者生活津貼) is only HK$2,565 per month and the Old Age Allowance (生果金) is only HK$1,290 per month.

見人講話合法居留夠5年就可以升做BC - answer is yes and no - yes, BNO passport holders can register to become a British Citizen after 5 years residence in the UK under section 4(2) of the British Nationality Act 1981 using form B(OTA). However, in section 4(2)(c), you need to have Indefinite Leave to Remain in the UK (ILR) for at lease 12 months in order to register. BNO passport holders can get ILR in the following ways -

1) Tier 1 investor (for example, you can get ILR after 3 years if you invest £5 million)
2) Tier 2 (General) - settlement (ILR) in 5 years.
3) Spouse visa - settlement in 5 years
4) Service in British Armed Forces - ILR in 4 years (immigration rule 276O)

有BNO 去英國讀公立大學係咪都要比$ - yes, even British Citizens need to pay university fees! 今天一名在囚已18年的香港永久居民李先生(BNO)，剛從外面的醫院做完切除腎臟的手術回來，他是在今年年初已被確診患上了癌症，而他本身又負擔不起任何的醫療費用，幸好他是持BNO的英國二等公民，所以英國駐港總領使館及英國駐菲律賓使館便出心出力的協助他，使館首先把李先生的問題向監獄反映，促使監獄部門要正視及責無旁貸地負責李先生的醫療問題，監獄部門起初也只是採用拖字訣，但由於在英國使館多次的施壓所以監獄部門不得不正視李先生的問題，在多次監獄及使館的商討後，菲政府終於給予了李先生免費的醫療服務(手術)，而使館更向李先生提供了藥物的津貼5萬Peso=8000港幣 。使館此項的藥物津貼，其實是使館服務範圍以外的協助，但為何使館仍會向李先生提供此服務呢？ 這完全是因為兩個字 “人道“ 。

吳先生，張先生
他們兩位是在囚已17年的中國香港永久居民，吳先生已患上了小腸氣多年，而張先生近年也常感覺到自己的消化系統有問題，懷疑自己患上了腸癌，由於他們兩人也是負擔不起任何的醫療費用，所以他們很希望港府及中國政府能為他們向菲律賓的監獄部門反映他們的問題，他們早前已寫信返港告訴向他們的家人，及他們的家人已多次致電香港入境處，協助在外居民小組，希望入境處能通知中國駐菲使館的職員前往監獄探訪他們，及向菲監獄部門反映他們的健康問題，結果呢.........從本年三四月份至今天，中國使館仍未有職員來探訪過他們 。其實他們倆在這17年的在囚時間裏，中國使館只曾探訪過他們不超過兩次 。香港入境處所謂的中國領使服務，其中一項就是每年起碼有一次的探訪，但事實郤是沒有，我及很多在囚的華人也很明白，中國使館的工作是非常煩多的，而每名在使館裏工作的中國外交部職員，也是出心出力的為人民服務，我想他們沒有來監獄裏探訪吳張二人，是因為香港入境處的疏忽 。

有無巴打做緊依行呀?
環境如何?


Is the Hedge Fund willing to sponsor you for a Tier 2 (General) visa? As far as I know that only Tier 2 (General) visa holder can get Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) [永久居留] in the UK after 5 years. Tier 2 (Inter-company Transfer) visa holders will not get ILR after 5 years. Once you have ILR for 12 months, you can apply for British Citizenship.

If you are going to move to London for the Hedge Fund job, you should use your BN(O) passport to apply for your Tier 2 (General) visa.

環境如何 - taxes are high and you take home pay is likely to be lower than what you have in Hong Kong. However, you got a chance to move to another country to experience life here and get British Citizenship at the end.

Scotland is a bit different - priority will be given to those who are willing to be train to teach in Catholic secondary schools.

The Scottish Government expects the School of Education to provide numbers of teachers to teach in Catholic schools in Scotland. This particular programme provides support for, and emphasis on, the initial teacher education of those who intend to teach in Catholic schools. The design of the programme necessarily reflects the denominational sector requirements and prospective applicants should consider this before applying. The School welcomes applications from all qualified candidates. In the selection procedures for interview, priority is given to those who indicated, in their personal statement, their intention to teach in Catholic schools and to seek the Catholic Teacher's Certificate in Religious Education.

(Source - The University of Glasgow - https://www.gla.ac.uk/postgraduate/taught/educationsecondary/)

我係郵局拎左form exchange driving license啦,佢話要收左我香港果張同埋係唔會比番我 - thats normal practice - you are 'exchanging' your Hong Kong driving licence to an UK one without test, so you need to hand back your Hong Kong licence. If you don't want to hand over your Hong Kong licence, you can sit and pass a driving test in the UK to get your UK driving licence. You cannot have your cake and eat it!

咁我如果番香港要渣既話點算 - A recent court judgement in Hong Kong dealt with your question. In HKSAR v. TSUI PAK SUN, PAXTON AND ANOTHER, HCMA 50/2016, the judge wrote that -

This is a simple question of fact as far as evidence is concerned and it is also a clear and unequivocal manifestation of such a permanent resident’s real intention as to whether he or she should be regarded as a “permanent resident” or a “visitor”. In those cases where the Hong Kong Permanent Identity Card holders still chose to enter Hong Kong by their foreign passports, I judge it would be wrong and illogical to disregard their conscious decisions and not to treat them as “visitors” as such.

If you are a Hong Kong permanent resident enter Hong Kong using your Hong Kong permanent ID card, then you cannot drive in Hong Kong as a visitor using your UK driving licence. On the other hand, if you are a British Citizen with a Hong Kong permanent resident and enter Hong Kong using your British Citizen passport, then you should be treated as a visitor for driving licences purposes in Hong Kong and therefore can drive in Hong Kong using your UK driving licence.
